pattern: Each alphabetic character independently doubles the choices, so enumerating every combination of letter cases is a product over the letters, naturally produced by recursive branching or by indexing the bits of a counter.
hints: Digits are fixed, but each letter offers two independent choices; the total count is two to the power of the number of letters.
Build the strings position by position: copy non-letters straight through, and at each letter branch into both the lowercase and uppercase variant.
Recurse over the index, accumulating a prefix; when you hit a letter, recurse once with its lowercase form and once with its uppercase form, and collect prefixes when the index reaches the end.

edge_inputs.description: two letters and two digits give four variants
single letter surrounded by digits gives two variants
single digit, only one possible string
all digits, no branching at all
a letter that is its own uppercase has no effect by char but still branches

stress.description: random short strings mixing letters and digits within the length cap
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 12)
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
stress.note: length kept within 12 so the output size stays bounded
